title: Object 60

{{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 60: ''"Corrosion Repellent"''}}
'''Object 60:'''
''"Corrosion Repellent"''
==Description== {{Blurimage|width=300px|align=right|file=Corrosion repellent 2.jpg|caption=A can of Corrosion Repellent. The photographer and location are entirely unknown, photo found taped on one of the walls of [[Level 1]].}} Corrosion Repellent is a highly elusive and valuable industrial substance due to its rarity to obtain, and if it is easy to obtain, the environment in which it is found in is often very dangerous. It is typically stored in industrial-grade spray cans that are completely blank, lacking branding, labels, or instructions. The cans appear to be made of a lightweight aluminum alloy, resistant to decay, such as rust or acids. The cans seem to have a somewhat high air pressure within itself, so it is highly recommended to take caution if one desires to experiment on the pure substance. The substance inside is an opaque, greenish-gray liquid with an unusually thin and watery consistency, almost appearing diluted. However, when sprayed or applied, it reacts immediately with the surface or substance, frothing up into a thick, foamy layer that rapidly expands in volume. This process resembles a soap-like lather, however the foam quickly dissipates once wiped away. The foam is often white when sprayed, however some substances/surfaces often cause the foam to change color. It seems to have no extremely harmful effect if ingested or applied onto oneself. In fact, Corrosion Repellent is one of the only known treatments against Ferrumasthenes Automatametallosis, more commonly and simply known as Ironlung Disease. ==Usage== The main usage for this substance is to reverse rust and corrosion from metals. Once applied to any metal, it froths up and forms a foamy lather. Once wiped off, it seems to remove all signs of rust from the metal. This effect can be replicated for metal panels or surfaces for up to 5 inches in thickness. For the Corrosion Repellent to fully take effect on the surface that it was designated to, it must be left to foam up on the surface for about 1-2 seconds at the very least. Corrosion Repellent may also reverse the effects of tarnish on other metals and materials. When applied, it also forms a very thin, nearly invisible film, that seems to keep the metal nearly impervious to oxidation or tarnish for a far longer amount of time, however the exact amount of time it protects it for is unknown, but estimated to protect the metal for around 10-15 years before signs of rush or tarnish begin to appear. Corrosion Repellent has a very widely known reputation to reverse the rust, tarnish and any other forms of corrosion on metals and other surfaces, but its lesser known purpose is to reverse the chemical reactions of various different materials. Many other reactions have been observed, such as being able to unbake bread, and when applied on plastic, seems to grow the plastic in volume, with the foam turning into plastic once applied. It has also reportedly formed unknown substances when mixed with a variety of substances, such as glass, porcelain, and trombone sliding grease. It has another notable property, as when mixed with either gallium or sodium, it turns into [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. ==Locations== It is often found in industrial-esque levels, such as [[Level 2]], [[Level 3]], and [[Level 3.53]]. It is however, extremely rare to find in most of levels. It is more commonly found in [[Level 3.53]]. It is also often sold in various amounts, or bid off in bulk in a variety of levels by The Scrap Barons. The Scrap Barons are a relatively friendly group, who mainly strive only for business, and don't prefer to be hostile, therefore it is not discouraged to purchase such items from them.
